page: The Microsoft Azure DS14-4_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA. It is equipped with 112.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 28.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 229 GB of local storage. No GPUs are bundled with this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between compute and memory capabilities. The server exhibits weak CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics, Geekbench scores, and PassMark CPU scores all falling into the bottom 25%. However, its memory subsystem performs in the average range, showing moderate memory bandwidth speeds of up to 359.24 GB/sec for cached reads and average PassMark memory scores. Database operations and Redis SET operations also achieve average performance tiers, with Redis handling 6.03 million operations per second. Large language model inference is limited, achieving average speeds on small 135M models but dropping to poor performance on medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DS14-4_v2 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a specialized choice where RAM capacity is prioritized over raw processing speed. This resource distribution makes the server suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require intensive CPU scaling, such as medium-sized caching layers, in-memory database operations, and memory-bound data processing tasks. It is less suited for compute-heavy applications, web serving, or complex machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure DS14-4_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the DSv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 112.0 GB of RAM and 229 GB of local storage. No GPU is included. While benchmark results indicate weak compute performance across single-core and multi-core CPU tests, the instance maintains average memory bandwidth and database operation speeds. This hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff, offering high memory capacity per core (28.0 GB) alongside modest processing capabilities. The DS14-4_v2 is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as caching, in-memory databases, and basic data processing where RAM capacity is the primary constraint.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS14-4_v2 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and 229 GB local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute needs.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS14-4_v2 is an x86_64 VM with 4 Intel vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and 229 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for memory-bound database and caching workloads.

112.0 GB of system memory providing 28.0 GB per core

Average performance in memory bandwidth and database operations
